victim.value: Keith and Jaqueline Perry, who befriended Mr Shaw for his money rather than genuine friendship
victim.quote: they were not real friends at all, they were just befriending him for his money

culprit_and_motive.value: Mr Shaw (the ghost tourist) seeking to decide whether to forgive Keith and Jaqueline for their deception
culprit_and_motive.quote: Could he forgive them though? he had asked himself... he began to appear to them as a tourist

resolution.value: Mr Shaw decides to forgive them, indicated by a snake with 'FORGIVEN' written on it
resolution.quote: written on the serpent in bold white lettering was the word: 'FORGIVEN'

investigator.value: Keith and Jaqueline Perry observing and eventually confronting the mysterious tourist
investigator.quote: Keith and Jaqueline looked at each other, then at him, then began to walk towards him

red_herring.value: The mysterious tourist appearing across multiple locations creates false supernatural mystery when the truth is personal guilt

clue_fairness.value: All clues available to Keith and Jaqueline are shown to reader: tourist appearances, identical clothing, weather immunity
clue_fairness.quote: nobody else was looking at him

social_world.value: Wealthy benefactor Mr Shaw and false friends exploiting his generosity for inheritance money
social_world.quote: they were not real friends at all, they were just befriending him for his money

reveal_mechanic.value: Supernatural confrontation in ice cave where Mr Shaw reveals his identity and makes forgiveness decision
reveal_mechanic.quote: In the ice cave in the arctic, he made his decision

media_and_audience.value: Story presents as supernatural mystery to both characters and readers until final revelation
media_and_audience.quote: There was nothing to say. They knew they would never see the tourist again

investigator_wound.value: Keith and Jaqueline's guilt over exploiting Mr Shaw's friendship for financial gain
investigator_wound.quote: Could he forgive them though? he had asked himself
